requested_as: User asked for 'Envive'; the live widget on Supergoop is actually Spiffy.ai (commerce-api.spiffy.ai, all spiffy-* shadow-DOM components). Labeled Spiffy.ai per user decision.
page_tested: PLAY Everyday Lotion SPF 50 product page
transport: Same-page shadow-DOM widgets (spiffy-modal-container etc.); same-origin, transcript readable directly. Answers render atomically (no visible token streaming).
metric: time to first substantive content (firstMs); since render is atomic this ≈ full-answer latency

capabilities.quick_replies: TWO kinds. (1) Inline PDP suggestion bars (top + bottom of product page): 'Summarize the reviews', 'Is it gentle enough for sensitive skin?', 'What do reviewers say about its texture?', 'Is this great for outdoor sports?'. (2) Dynamic suggested follow-up chips after EVERY chat answer, contextual to the last reply.
capabilities.product_cards: Rich 'pull cards' — image + name + price (From $X) + star rating + review count. ~3 tailored cards per discovery turn; respects budget + use-case constraints.
capabilities.reviews: Deepest review integration of any vendor. Surfaces aggregate rating (4.7/5, 3,400+), summarizes reviews on demand, and on an objection turn cited specific named reviewers (Starla P., Lupe, Helen M.) with verified-buyer quotes addressing the exact concern (oily skin / no breakout).
capabilities.bundles: Recommended the 'Best of Supergoop! SPF Set' ($134) when asked for better value.
capabilities.add_to_cart: Claims to add items to the bag and offers a 'ready to checkout' chip (cart-sync present); for the total it defers to the cart page.
capabilities.shipping_info: Gave free-shipping threshold ($50) + a gift-with-purchase ($70+) directly in chat.
capabilities.modes: Two tabs: 'Shop with AI' (shopping) + 'Customer Care Team' (support).
capabilities.handover: Zero handovers across all 10 normal shopping turns — answered everything itself.

turns.q: Hi! I'm not sure which sunscreen is right for me — can you help me pick one?
turns.a: Clarifying question (water resistance? SPF level?) + 5 follow-up chips (water-resistant / high SPF sensitive / lightweight / natural scent / daily use).

turns.q: I have oily, acne-prone skin, outdoors a lot for running, hate the greasy white-cast.
turns.a: 3 product cards + follow-up chips ('Which one is best for running?').

turns.q: Keep it under ~$40, ideally also works under makeup.
Compare Unseen Sunscreen vs Mineral Mattescreen for oily skin & running — which is better?
turns.a: Unseen = weightless/invisible, doubles as primer, chemical; Mattescreen = mattifies, blurs pores, controls shine, mineral. + cards + a verified-buyer review quote (Chasity 5.0).

turns.q: Worried it'll feel heavy and break me out — what do reviewers with oily skin actually say?
turns.a: REVIEW-DRIVEN: cites Starla P. (silky, light, not oily), Lupe (doesn't break her out), Helen M. (no oily residue) + a verified-buyer quote. Card: Unseen $38 ★4.7 (5651).

turns.q: I also need something for my face for daily wear under makeup — what works?
Is there a set or bundle with both that's better value?
turns.a: Recommended the Best of Supergoop! SPF Set as a better-value bundle + chips.

turns.q: Which one is your overall best seller, and is it well reviewed?
turns.a: Best seller = Unseen Sunscreen SPF 50 (invisible, weightless, blends under makeup) + chips.

turns.q: Add the Unseen Sunscreen and the Mineral Mattescreen to my bag.
turns.a: Confirms it added both to the bag + offers 'I'm ready to checkout now!' / 'What's the return policy?' chips.

turns.q: What's my total, and is there free shipping or a first-order discount?
turns.a: Defers total to the cart page; states free shipping over $50 and a free Glow Stick at $70+.
